title: One shared Express MCP handler factory replaces 14 copy-pasted createXMcpHandler functions
description: Every *Mcp.ts file ends with an identical ~20-line Express handler: POST-method check, read x-agent-id/x-board-id headers, new StreamableHTTPServerTransport({sessionIdGenerator: undefined}), createXMcpServer(...), connect, handleRequest, catch-log-500. 14 literal copies: gmailMcp.ts:1003-1025, outlookMcp.ts:849-870, gdriveMcp.ts:619-639, wordpressMcp.ts:528-548, githubMcp.ts:524-544, jiraMcp.ts:403-423, onedriveMcp.ts:388-410, slackMcp.ts:378-399, s3Mcp.ts:286-307, swarmApiMcp.ts:663-685, codeIndexMcp.ts:333-351, autoLearnMcp.ts:168-186, gandiDnsMcp.ts:147-165, browserMcp.ts:103-121. The only variations are the log label and which header values the server builder consumes. Any fix to this boilerplate (e.g. a header or error-shape change) currently requires 14 edits.
impact: ~280 lines of boilerplate collapse to a ~25-line factory plus 14 one-to-three-line wrappers (net -200 lines across 14 files); the request-bridging convention lives in exactly one place
guidance: Sketch is correct as designed; respect these traps when implementing: (1) Header values from Express are string | string[]; current code passes the raw value through with || null and NO array normalization. Keep the `as string` cast (or pass raw) — do not "fix" repeated headers with Array.isArray(...)[0], which would change what builders receive. The || null also maps empty-string headers to null today; preserve it. (2) swarmApiMcp.ts:670-672 has a meaningful comment (X-Agent-Id injected for CLI runner agents, absent for external API-key callers) — move it onto the wrapper, and note its builder takes (agentManager, callerAgentId) ignoring boardId; the factory reading x-board-id anyway is side-effect-free and fine. (3) codeIndex/autoLearn/gandi/browser builders take no agent context — their wrappers should just ignore ctx, not grow new params. (4) Keep gmailMcp's JSDoc about runnerBridge (gmailMcp.ts:993-1002) on its wrapper, and keep the runnerBridge: RunnerExecBridge | null = null default on gmail and outlook wrappers so index.ts:226-234 stays untouched. (5) Routes are mounted with app.all (index.ts:220-272), so the 405-for-non-POST branch is live behavior — keep it first, before the try. (6) Error response must remain res.status(500).json({ error: err.message }) guarded by !res.headersSent, logged as `[<Label> MCP] Error:` — labels differ per file ('Gmail', 'Gdrive', 'WordPress', 'Swarm API', 'Code Index', 'Auto Learn', 'Gandi DNS', etc.); copy each file's existing label exactly to keep log greppability. (7) Remove the now-unused StreamableHTTPServerTransport (and any RunnerExecBridge-only-for-handler) imports per file so api tsc stays clean.

title: Shared provider-fetch factory for the 7 near-identical {gmail,graph,drive,github,jira,wp}Fetch helpers
description: Seven files each define a private fetch helper with the same skeleton: resolve per-agent credentials, prefix base URL unless the path starts with 'http', fetch with AbortSignal.timeout(60_000), spread options then merge Authorization + Content-Type headers, throw `<Provider> API error <status>: <text>` on !res.ok, then parse JSON-or-text by content-type. Copies: gmailFetch (gmailMcp.ts:82-106), graphFetch (outlookMcp.ts:83-114, adds 202/204→null), graphFetch again (onedriveMcp.ts:22-46, 120s timeout), driveFetch (gdriveMcp.ts:22-55, adds raw mode + conditional Content-Type), githubFetch (githubMcp.ts:8-34, extra Accept/User-Agent/Api-Version headers), jiraFetch (jiraMcp.ts:9-35, Basic auth + 204→null + different arg order), wpFetch (wordpressMcp.ts:41-74, Basic auth + creds-missing error + conditional Content-Type). Two of them are even both named graphFetch with different timeouts. Conventions (timeout, error truncation, empty-status handling) have silently drifted between copies.
impact: ~210 lines of drifting copies become one ~45-line factory + 7 short configs (net ~-120 lines, 7 files); timeout/error/parse conventions become one decision instead of seven
guidance: Corrections to the sketch (each is a behavior-preservation trap if implemented literally): (1) jira/wp base URLs are dynamic per-agent, not static opts.base — jiraFetch builds `https://${creds.domain}` (jiraMcp.ts:13) and wpFetch builds `${creds.siteUrl}/wp-json` with leading-slash normalization (wordpressMcp.ts:50-52); change getAuthHeader to getAuth(agentId, boardId) → { authorization, base } and keep wp's slash handling. (2) jira/wp always res.json() after the 204→null check (jiraMcp.ts:33-34, wordpressMcp.ts:72-73), they do NOT do content-type-based json-or-text like the other five — add a parse: 'json' | 'contentType' option. (3) jira/wp error paths use res.text().catch(() => '') and truncate at slice(0,300) / slice(0,400); the others use untrimmed await res.text() with no catch — preserve via per-provider maxErrorChars (or unify deliberately, but don't call it preservation). (4) outlook's error message is `Microsoft Graph error ${status}` and onedrive's is `Graph API error ${status}` — the `${name} API error` template breaks both; pass the full error label per provider instead. (5) Do not hardcode 'Content-Type': 'application/json' in the factory: githubFetch sends no default Content-Type at all, and gdrive/wp only set it when options.body is a string and the caller didn't provide one (gdriveMcp.ts:35-37, wordpressMcp.ts:62-64 — critical for WP media uploads); the sketch's jsonContentTypeOnlyWithBody flag is declared but never used in the body — actually wire it as a three-way policy (always / onlyStringBody / none). Minor: the `options.raw ? 120_000` timeout is a gdrive-only convention (onedrive needs timeoutMs: 120_000 unconditionally; only gdriveMcp.ts:152,161 pass raw), and jiraFetch/wpFetch keep their (agentId, boardId, path, options) arg order via one-line reordering wrappers. Net effect after corrections: the factory needs ~6 config knobs, slightly less slim than sketched (~60 lines, not 45), but still a clear win over seven drifting copies.

title: Extract shared email-attachment module duplicated byte-for-byte between gmailMcp and outlookMcp
description: gmailMcp.ts and outlookMcp.ts each carry a ~250-line attachment stack that is identical except for two constants and the final wire shape: RunnerExecBridge type (gmail 14-20 / outlook 20-26), the 40-entry MIME_BY_EXT map + guessMimeType (gmail 33-76 / outlook 35-78), shQuote (gmail 227-230 / outlook 116-118), readAttachmentViaRunner with the probe-then-base64 protocol (gmail 241-298 / outlook 120-168 — only MAX_ATTACHMENT_BYTES 20MB vs 3MB and maxOutput 32MiB vs 8MiB differ), the two-tier resolveAttachment local-fs-then-runner fallback (gmail 312-391 / outlook 184-266), and attachmentsSchema (gmail 513-523 / outlook 268-278). wordpressMcp.ts:12-36 carries a third, smaller MIME_BY_EXT + guessMimeType copy. The security-sensitive probe/fallback logic (cross-agent isolation, truncation detection) is maintained in two places; the Gmail copy already accumulated fixes (e.g. the maxOutput truncation comment at gmail 274-277) that had to be mirrored manually.
impact: Removes ~280 duplicated lines (net) across 3 files; the cross-agent file-read security logic and the MIME table get a single owner
guidance: Two corrections to the sketch: (1) The description's claim "identical except for two constants and the final wire shape" misses a real asymmetry in resolveAttachment's `content` branch: outlookMcp.ts:256-259 strips whitespace and validates base64 (throwing 'Attachment "X" content is not valid base64.') at resolve time, while gmailMcp.ts:382-391 returns content raw and performs the identical clean+validate later in buildRawEmail (gmailMcp.ts:481-484). The shared resolveAttachmentInput must return the content-branch payload RAW; the outlook adapter does clean+validate, and gmail keeps its existing validation in buildRawEmail. Do not unify validation into the shared helper, or gmail's error timing/origin shifts. (2) Drop wordpressMcp from this refactor (or treat it as a separate, explicitly behavior-changing follow-up): its private map (wordpressMcp.ts:12-32) has only 19 entries vs the shared 40; importing the shared guessMimeType changes the Content-Type sent to WordPress for ~18 extensions (.md, .html, .docx, .xlsx, .ppt, .odt, .gz, .tar, .7z, .ics...) from application/octet-stream to specific types, which can flip WordPress's media-upload accept/reject behavior. Remaining traps to respect: limitLabel must be applied at BOTH size-limit error sites — the runner probe (gmailMcp.ts:267-272 vs outlookMcp.ts:144-149) AND the local-fs tier-1 stat check (gmailMcp.ts:343-347 vs outlookMcp.ts:214-218) — preserving exact strings: gmail ends '...MB limit.' and outlook '...MB inline-attachment limit for Outlook.'. buildAttachmentsSchema's describe strings are wire-visible in the MCP tools/list response: per-field describes are identical between the two files, but outlook's array-level describe appends 'Outlook inline-attachment limit ≈ 3 MB per file.' (outlookMcp.ts:278) — keep via the extraDescription parameter. Keep the probe command and timeouts (10000/120000) byte-identical. RunnerExecBridge is consumed at api/src/index.ts:226-234 where the handlers are constructed, so export the type from the new module and update both handler signatures. No test coverage exists for any of this code, so review the extraction diff carefully — tsc is the only gate.

title: mcpManager: merge _callToolWithAgentAuth/_callToolWithAgentContext and extract the call-retry-shape pattern (copied 3x)
description: Three methods repeat the same 'callTool → if message contains 404/session/Invalid token/token/401 then drop client, reconnect, retry once' block: callTool (mcpManager.ts:466-496), _callToolWithAgentAuth (576-609), _callToolWithAgentContext (630-663). _callToolWithAgentAuth and _callToolWithAgentContext are line-for-line identical except the cacheKey and the headers object — even the comments match. The 4-line result-shaping block ({ success: !result.isError, result: extracted.text, images, raw }) is pasted 6 times (469-474, 484-489, 579-584, 601-606, 633-638, 655-660), and the fragile session-expiry sniffing condition is pasted 3 times (476, 587, 641). Any change to the retry policy or expiry heuristic requires 3 coordinated edits today.
impact: mcpManager.ts shrinks ~853 → ~710 lines; retry policy, expiry heuristic, and result shape each have exactly one definition instead of 3/3/6
guidance: 1) The sketch's claim "Covered by mcpManager.internal.test.ts" is FALSE — that file only tests resolveInternalMcpConfig, builtin-server registration, and skill lists; no existing test exercises callTool, the agent call paths, or the retry logic. Verify by careful diff and consider adding a small unit test for _isSessionExpired and the _callAgentClient retry path (stub agentClients/_connectAgentClient). 2) The sketch drops both reconnect console.log lines ("Agent session/token expired..." / "Agent context session expired..."); keep a log line in _callAgentClient (a unified message or a label param) — service logs are read operationally in this deployment. 3) Keep _isSessionExpired semantically identical to the original err.message?.includes chain over exactly ['404','session','Invalid token','token','401']; do not prune the redundant 'Invalid token' entry in the same change. 4) Do NOT route callTool through _callAgentClient: it uses this.clients (not agentClients), reconnects via this.connect(serverId) (which mutates server.status and persists), falls through to rethrowing the ORIGINAL err when no retry client exists, and wraps retry failures as `MCP call failed after reconnect: ${retryErr.message}` — only _shape and _isSessionExpired are shared there. 5) Preserve the exact context cacheKey format `${agentId}:${server.id}${boardId ? `:${boardId}` : ''}` and the boardId=null default on the public-facing signature of callToolByNameForAgent (unchanged).

title: Single source of truth for internal MCP servers (URL map + hardcoded OAuth-server boolean chain)
description: The set of internal MCP servers is encoded three times in parallel: (1) BUILTIN_MCP_SERVERS in data/mcpServers.ts declares each `__internal__*` url; (2) resolveInternalMcpConfig hardcodes a 15-entry `__internal__x → http://localhost:PORT/api/x/mcp` map (mcpManager.ts:25-41); (3) callToolByNameForAgent hardcodes which servers need per-agent context in a 8-clause boolean chain on one line (mcpManager.ts:547: server.url === '__internal__onedrive' || ... || '__internal__github'). Adding a new internal MCP requires touching all three, and forgetting (3) silently breaks per-agent OAuth (the past 'decide looped' incidents were this class of gap). The route paths also already exist a fourth time in index.ts:220-272.
impact: Deletes the 15-line literal map and the unreadable 1-line/8-clause condition; new internal MCPs become a one-entry change instead of three scattered edits
guidance: Corrections to the sketch: (1) Do NOT add internalPath/agentContext directly onto BUILTIN_MCP_SERVERS entries as-is — builtin defs are spread into runtime server objects that are persisted to the mcp_servers JSONB blob and returned over REST: ensureBuiltinServerRegistered {...builtin}+saveMcpServer (mcpManager.ts:148-158), seedDefaults new-server branch (191-200), and getAll/getById via createBuiltinServerEntry (74-83, 273-286). That adds fields to DB rows and API responses (wire-format change). Either export a sibling map in data/mcpServers.ts keyed by __internal__ url ({ path, agentContext }) next to BUILTIN_MCP_SERVERS, or strip the two new fields at those three spread sites. (2) internalPath must remain explicit per entry — paths are not derivable from url slugs (__internal__aws_s3 → /api/s3/mcp; underscore→dash for code-index/gandi-dns/swarm-api/auto-learn). (3) The test net is weaker than claimed: mcpManager.internal.test.ts pins only code_index and onedrive URLs; aws_s3, the dash alias __internal__code-index, and the entire agentContext set at line 547 are untested. Transcribe the agentContext set exactly as {onedrive, gmail, outlook, gdrive, slack, jira, wordpress, github} — the other 6 internal servers (code_index, gandi_dns, swarm_api, aws_s3, auto_learn, browser) must keep using the global connection. Consider extending the test to pin aws_s3 and the dash alias. (4) Keep URL construction inside resolveInternalMcpConfig using the port param (tests pass port 4123) — don't precompute full URLs at module load. (5) Benign semantic delta to be aware of: mappings[serverUrl] object lookup was truthy for prototype keys ('constructor', 'toString'); Map.get returns undefined → passthrough. Latent-bug fix, acceptable. (6) Leave the index.ts:220-272 route mounts (the 4th copy) alone — handlers take heterogeneous constructor args; unifying them would be over-abstraction.

title: swarmApiMcp: jsonError/jsonOk helpers + shared agent-resolution kill ~15 repeated MCP-response blocks
description: The 5-line error envelope `{ content: [{ type: 'text', text: JSON.stringify({ error: ... }) }], isError: true }` is pasted ~12 times (swarmApiMcp.ts:108-115, 210-217, 225-233, 236-243, 251-261, 272-279, 355-359, 365-372, 380-409, 420-427, 545-549, 627-634), and the success envelope `JSON.stringify(..., null, 2)` another ~8 times. Resolving an agent from agent_id-or-agent_name (Array.from(agentManager.agents.values()).find(name-lowercase-match)) is copied 3 times: get_agent_status (99-105), update_task (313-318), search_tasks (540-551). The actual tool logic is buried under envelope plumbing — update_task is 200 lines of which ~70 are response wrappers.
impact: ~80 lines removed in one file; every tool body reads as validation + action instead of envelope assembly
guidance:
Corrections and traps for the implementer:
1. Counts: 14 plain error envelopes (not ~12) and 6 clean success envelopes (not ~8). The reviewer's "545-549" is 544-549 and "380-409" spans three separate blocks — recount when editing.
2. task_execution_complete (swarmApiMcp.ts:642-653) is NOT a plain envelope: it has `...(outcome.success ? {} : { isError: true })`. Leave it as-is, or compose as `outcome.success ? jsonOk(p) : { ...jsonOk(p), isError: true as const }` — do not run it through plain jsonOk/jsonError.
3. search_tasks (539-551) does NOT validate agent_id — an unknown agent_id is passed straight to searchTasks and returns zero results, not an error; only the agent_name branch errors on miss. Use findAgent there only for the name branch (`if (!resolvedAgentId && agent_name)`); routing agent_id through findAgent and erroring on null would change behavior. So findAgent fully replaces only get_agent_status (99-105) and update_task (313-318).
4. update_task's no-params branch (319-325) scans all agents for the task. Today a provided-but-unknown agent_id skips that scan and falls through to the DB lookup at 335-353. Keep the scan gated on "neither agent_id nor agent_name provided" — do not turn it into a fallback after findAgent returns null.
5. The 405/500 responses in createSwarmApiMcpHandler (666, 681) are HTTP JSON, not MCP envelopes — out of scope.
6. TypeScript: inline literals are contextually typed by the SDK callback signature; extracted helpers lose that. Keep `type: 'text' as const` (and `isError: true as const`) as sketched and confirm assignability to the installed @modelcontextprotocol/sdk CallToolResult before declaring done (run api tsc).
7. Error strings are regex-asserted by tests (/Invalid repo_full_name/, /Task not found/, /Agent not found/, /board_id is required/, /At least one of/, /not bound to a board/, /No agent context/) — every message must stay byte-identical, which holds as long as messages are passed verbatim to jsonError.

title: swarmApiMcp: decompose the 200-line update_task handler and dedupe board-column validation with add_task
description: update_task (swarmApiMcp.ts:310-509) does five jobs inline: agent resolution (313-325), task lookup with DB-fallback + in-memory rehydration setting a `boardLevel` flag (327-360), three validation blocks, then two fully divergent mutation paths — a 40-line board-level branch that hand-rolls history entries and execution-state clearing (448-486) versus the agentManager helper path (487-497). The status-against-board-columns validation (378-410) is a near-copy of the same check in add_task (249-262), including the identical `Invalid status "x" for board ... Valid columns:` error string. The boardLevel boolean threading makes the control flow hard to follow: the reader must track which of agent/task/boardLevel combinations is live at each point.
sketch:
Extract three module-level helpers:
async function validateStatusForBoard(boardId: string, status: string): Promise<{ ok: true } | { ok: false; error: string }>  // shared by add_task and update_task
async function locateTask(agentManager, { agent_id, agent_name, task_id }): Promise<{ task; agent: Agent | null; boardLevel: boolean } | null>  // the 327-360 rehydration dance
async function applyBoardLevelUpdate(agentManager, task, { repoUpdate, storageUpdate, status }): Promise<Task>  // the 448-486 branch incl. history push + execution-state reset + _checkAutoRefine hook
update_task then reads: locate → validate inputs → validate status → (boardLevel ? applyBoardLevelUpdate : apply via agentManager helpers) → jsonOk. swarmApiMcp.test.ts pins both paths.
impact: update_task drops from ~200 to ~80 lines; the duplicated column-validation error logic becomes one function; the board-level vs in-memory split becomes explicit instead of flag-threaded
guidance: 1) Drop or narrow the validateStatusForBoard dedup: the two blocks are semantically divergent. add_task (swarmApiMcp.ts:249) skips validation when the board has no workflow columns and when status is falsy (incl. ''); update_task (378-398) hard-errors on missing task.boardId, hard-errors on missing columns, and validates '' (status !== undefined gating). If deduping at all, share only the genuinely identical inner part — e.g. findInvalidStatusError(board, boardLabelId, status): string | null doing the case-insensitive column match + the identical `Invalid status "x" for board "y". Valid columns: ...` string — taking the already-fetched board object (add_task has it from line 235; do not re-fetch) and keeping all gating at each call site. Do not unify with the similar-but-differently-worded checks in agentManager/tools.ts:592-624 or routes/tasks.ts:270 (wire-visible error strings differ). 2) The sketch's claim that swarmApiMcp.test.ts pins both paths is false: the boardLevel branch (448-486) has zero test coverage — the test file never mocks ./database/tasks.js getTaskById and its fake agentManager defines no saveTaskDirectly/_emit/_checkAutoRefine/_ensureTaskInMemory. Either add a pin test first (mock getTaskById to return an agentId-less task; extend the fake manager) or keep applyBoardLevelUpdate a strict cut-paste. 3) Behavior traps in applyBoardLevelUpdate: preserve the two distinct history-entry shapes (edit entries {status, at, by, type:'edit', field, oldValue, newValue} vs status-change entry {from, status, at, by}); preserve that the _checkAutoRefine guard at line 483 reads task.status AFTER mutation (i.e. it checks the NEW status !== 'error'); preserve saveTaskDirectly receiving {...task, agentId: task.agentId || null} and the _emit before _checkAutoRefine ordering; keep status==='done' => completedAt. 4) Trap in locateTask: when agent was resolved via agent_id/agent_name but the task is absent from that agent's list, the DB fallback may rehydrate and REASSIGN agent to a different owner (line 341-344) — the helper must return the possibly-reassigned agent, and the final response at line 505 uses that agent's id/name.

title: gmailMcp: list_emails and search_emails share a duplicated fetch-metadata-and-format block
description: list_emails (gmailMcp.ts:584-611) and search_emails (629-654) both end with the same ~25-line sequence: Promise.all over messages.slice(0, limit) fetching `?format=metadata&metadataHeaders=From&...` per id, map through formatMessage, then build the numbered `${unread} ${subject} / From / Date / snippet / ID` summary. The only difference is that list_emails also renders a star emoji (602) and the header line text. The long metadata query string is duplicated verbatim (594 and 638).
impact: ~30 lines removed; the Gmail metadata-fields contract is stated once
guidance: Sketch is essentially correct; respect these traps: (1) Keep the exact template `${i + 1}. ${unread}${star} ${m.subject}` — do not "normalize" spacing (e.g. `${unread} ${star}${subject}`), or starred list_emails output changes; with star='' it already matches search_emails byte-for-byte. (2) The sketch's signature takes messageIds: string[] but both call sites hold message objects from `list.messages`; either pass `messages.map(m => m.id)` or accept `{id: string}[]`. Keep `slice(0, limit)` inside before fetching so the API call count is unchanged. (3) Keep the empty-list early returns and the distinct header lines ('Found N email(s)' vs 'Search "q" found N result(s)', both using `list.resultSizeEstimate || messages.length`) in the callers — they differ and the caller still needs `list`. (4) Do NOT fold in the third format=metadata fetch at line 758 (reply flow) — it requests a different header set (Cc, Message-ID, References) for a different purpose. (5) Do not "improve" search_emails by always rendering the star — that would change output bytes for starred search results; keep the withStar flag as sketched.

title: Shared size-capped response reader for gdriveMcp.downloadFile and onedriveMcp.read_file
description: The defensive 'stream the body via getReader(), accumulate chunks, abort with a too-large error if the running total exceeds maxBytes, with an arrayBuffer fallback when no reader exists' loop is implemented twice with the same structure and comments: gdriveMcp.ts:165-188 (inside downloadFile) and onedriveMcp.ts:184-208 (inside the read_file tool). Both even share the `try { await reader.cancel(); } catch { /* ignore */ }` line. This is non-obvious backpressure/heap-safety code that should have one owner.
impact: ~45 duplicated lines → one ~20-line helper; the heap-safety invariant is testable in isolation
guidance: One subtle divergence the sketch silently papers over: the two copies format the max-size portion of the error message differently. gdrive (gdriveMcp.ts:169,183) uses (maxBytes/1024/1024).toFixed(1) → "max 5.0 MB" / ">5.0 MB"; onedrive (onedriveMcp.ts:189,202) uses MAX_READ_BYTES/1024/1024 with no toFixed → "max 5 MB" / ">5 MB". The sketched helper changes onedrive's thrown error text (e.g. ">5 MB" → ">5.0 MB"). Verified no test asserts these strings and no code parses them (they surface only as MCP error text to the agent), so harmonizing on toFixed(1) is fine — but state the message change explicitly in the commit/PR rather than calling it byte-identical. Also: (1) onedrive's separate pre-download metadata size check at onedriveMcp.ts:156-166 returns a friendly content message instead of throwing — it must NOT be folded into the helper; the helper only replaces the post-download backstop, as the sketch already does. (2) llmProviders.ts:245 also calls getReader() but is SSE streaming — leave it out of scope. (3) Keep the helper signature (res: Response, maxBytes: number) => Promise<Buffer>; gdrive passes its maxBytes parameter (default MAX_TEXT_READ_BYTES), onedrive passes MAX_READ_BYTES.

title: slackApi: replace the inline GET-method string chain and triple fetch branches with a lookup set
description: slackMcp.ts:14 buries the GET-vs-POST decision in one boolean expression mixing a structural condition with five hardcoded method names: `const isGet = Object.keys(params).length === 0 || method === 'users.list' || method === 'conversations.list' || ...`. Lines 17-41 then spell out three nearly identical fetch calls (GET with query string, bare GET, POST) that differ only in URL suffix and body. A reader cannot tell at a glance which Slack methods are GET by design versus GET by accident of having no params, and adding a new read method means editing a boolean chain.
impact: 30 lines → ~18 with one fetch call instead of three; GET-by-design methods become declarative data
guidance: Three behavior-preservation traps: (1) The GET set must contain EXACTLY the five methods from line 14 — do NOT add conversations.replies even though it is a read method (read_thread, line 154); it currently goes out as POST with a JSON body and adding it would change the wire request. Likewise keep the zero-params-implies-GET quirk (`|| Object.keys(params).length === 0`). (2) Trailing-'?' edge: the original appends `?${qs}` whenever params is non-empty, even if all values are null/undefined (empty qs → URL ends in bare '?'); the sketch's `if (qs.size)` drops that '?'. HTTP-equivalent but not byte-identical — gate on `Object.keys(params).length > 0` like the original if exactness is wanted. (3) TS typing: `init.headers['Content-Type']` fails type-check on HeadersInit; declare headers as Record<string, string> and build the fetch init from it. Non-issues confirmed: || reordering is pure; URLSearchParams(entries) vs set() loop identical since Object.entries keys are unique; URLSearchParams.size is available (@types/node ^25); no test expectations exist for this module.
